mpipe/ingress/app.c

Use the gxio_mpipe_ API to receive ethernet packets.

// Copyright 2014 Tilera Corporation. All Rights Reserved.
//
//   The source code contained or described herein and all documents
//   related to the source code ("Material") are owned by Tilera
//   Corporation or its suppliers or licensors.  Title to the Material
//   remains with Tilera Corporation or its suppliers and licensors. The
//   software is licensed under the Tilera MDE License.
//
//   Unless otherwise agreed by Tilera in writing, you may not remove or
//   alter this notice or any other notice embedded in Materials by Tilera
//   or Tilera's suppliers or licensors in any way.
//
//

// ============================================================================
// mpipe/ingress/app.c -- mPIPE packet ingress example
// ============================================================================

// This example demonstrates using the mPIPE API
// to perform simple ingress of network packets.
//
// Packets are ingressed over a single network link,
// which defaults to "gbe1" but may be specified via
// the --link command-line argument.

// Standard C includes.
#include <stdio.h>          // printf(), etc.
#include <stdlib.h>         // calloc(), atoi()
#include <stdbool.h>        // bool type and values
#include <errno.h>          // errno
#include <string.h>         // strcmp(), strerror()
#include <assert.h>         // assert()

// Tilera includes.

// mPIPE API (part of GXIO library, -lgxio).
#include <gxio/mpipe.h>

// Tilera TMC library (-ltmc).
#include <tmc/task.h>       // tmc_task_die()
#include <tmc/cpus.h>       // cpu sets and affinity
#include <tmc/alloc.h>      // memory allocation

// Other Tilera-specific includes.
#include <sys/dataplane.h>  // ZOL (dataplane tile) mode options
#include <arch/sim.h>       // sim_enable_mpipe_links()


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// constants
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

#define VALIDATE_PACKETS

#define DEBUG_DATAPLANE


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// macros
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Align "p" mod "align", assuming "p" is a "void*".
#define ALIGN(p, align) \
  do { (p) += -(long)(p) & ((align) - 1); } while(0)


#define VERIFY_GXIO(VAL, WHAT)                                  \
  do {                                                          \
    long __val = (long) (VAL);                                  \
    if (__val < 0)                                              \
      tmc_task_die("%s: (%ld) %s.",                             \
                   (WHAT), __val, gxio_strerror(__val));        \
  } while (0)

#define VERIFY_ERRNO(VAL, WHAT)                                 \
  do {                                                          \
    long __val = (long) (VAL);                                  \
    if (__val == -1)                                            \
      tmc_task_die("%s: (%ld) %s.",                             \
                   (WHAT), __val, strerror(errno));             \
  } while (0)


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// globals
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// NOTE:
// This example has only one worker, so these don't _need_ to be globals,
// but in a multi-threaded application making these global is convenient
// for sharing, so we'll follow the same pattern to make it easier
// to extend this example if desired.


// Command-line options:

static char* link_name = NULL;

static int link_flags = 0;

static int num_packets = 1000;

static int cpu = -1;

static bool verbose = false;


// Internals:

static cpu_set_t cpu_set;

static cpu_set_t dataplane_cpu_set;

static bool is_dataplane = false;


// mPIPE data structures:

static int mpipe_instance;

static gxio_mpipe_context_t context_body;
static gxio_mpipe_context_t* const mpipe_context = &context_body;

static gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t iqueue_body;
static gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t* const iqueue = &iqueue_body;

static unsigned int iqueue_entries = 128; //GXIO_MPIPE_IQUEUE_ENTRY_128;


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// main_worker()
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void
main_worker()
{
  int result;

  // --- worker task initialization ---

  // Bind to single CPU.
  if (tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu(cpu) != 0)
    tmc_task_die("main_loop(): "
                 "Cannot affinitize to cpu %d.", cpu);

  // Note: in general running on a dataplane tile is preferred,
  // but this example supports running on a non-dataplane tile as well.
  // If we're on a tile in the dataplane set...
  if (is_dataplane)
  {
    // Lock thread's current virtual pages (code, data structures, etc.)
    // into physical memory, to minimize swapping overhead.
    mlockall(MCL_CURRENT);

    // If we had multiple workers, we'd want to use a spin barrier
    // here to ensure everyone's arrived before changing modes.

#ifdef DEBUG_DATAPLANE
    // Put ZOL tiles in "debug" mode -- if interrupts other than page faults
    // occur on this tile, a kernel stacktrace is generated.
    int result = set_dataplane(DP_DEBUG);
    VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "set_dataplane()");

    // Again, if we had multiple workers, we'd want to use a spin barrier
    // here to ensure everyone's changed modes before moving on.
#endif
  }

  // On the simulator, this enables simulation of network traffic.
  // On hardware, this is a no-op.
  sim_enable_mpipe_links(mpipe_instance, -1);


  // --- ingress packets ---

  // Process packets.
  int handled = 0;
  while (handled < num_packets || num_packets <= 0)
  {
    // Wait (blocking) for next packet.
    // Normally, a worker would spin waiting for packets,
    // and perhaps check other queues if there are none available.
    // For this example, we only have the one queue,
    // so we'll block until packets arrive, to keep things simple.
    gxio_mpipe_idesc_t idesc;
    result = gxio_mpipe_iqueue_get(iqueue, &idesc);
    VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_iqueue_get()");

    // NOTE: On the simulator, we run with ":stall", and so we will
    // never drop packets due to running out of buffers.  Otherwise,
    // we would need to specially handle packets with "idesc.be",
    // for example, by using "gxio_mpipe_iqueue_drop_if_bad()".

    // Analyze the packet descriptor.
    int size = idesc.l2_size;
    if (verbose)
      printf("Packet %d has size %d\n", handled, size);

#ifdef VALIDATE_PACKETS

    // MAC Error.
    // The default classifier drops such packets (via packet_has_error()).
    assert(!idesc.me);

    // Truncation Error.
    // The default classifier drops such packets (via packet_has_error()).
    assert(!idesc.tr);

    // CRC Error.
    // The default classifier drops such packets (via packet_has_error()).
    assert(!idesc.ce);

    // Cut through.
    // The default classifier drops such packets.
    assert(!idesc.ct);

    // Checksum.
    // The default classifier requests checksums for IPv4/IPv6 TCP/UDP
    // packets (except UDP packets whose udp checksum is zero).
    assert(idesc.cs && idesc.csum_seed_val == 0xffff);

    // Notification Ring Select (vs let the load balancer pick a ring).
    // The default classifier always uses 0.
    assert(!idesc.nr);

    // Destination (0 = drop, 1 = deliver, 2 = deliver only descriptor).
    // The default classifier only uses 0 and 1, and 0 causes drops.
    assert(idesc.dest == 1);

    // General Purpose Sequence Number Enable.
    // The default classifier currently uses 0.
    //--assert(idesc.sq);

    // Enable TimeStamp insertion.
    // The default classifier always uses 1.
    assert(idesc.ts);

    // Packet Sequence Number Enable.
    // The default classifier always uses 1.
    assert(idesc.ps);

    // Buffer Error.
    // ISSUE: This can happen if the buffer stack is empty.
    assert(!idesc.be);

    // The "vlan" (or 0xFFFF).
    uint16_t vlan = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_vlan(&idesc);
    assert(vlan == 0xFFFF);

    // The ethertype (or zero).  IPv4 = 0x0800, IPv6 = 0x86DD.
    uint16_t ethertype = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_ethertype(&idesc);
    assert(ethertype == 0x0800);

    // The byte index of the start of the l2 data.
    uint8_t l2_offset = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_l2_offset(&idesc);
    assert(l2_offset == 0);

    // The byte index of the start of the l3 header.
    uint8_t l3_offset = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_l3_offset(&idesc);
    assert(l3_offset >= l2_offset + 14);

    // The byte index of the start of the l4 header (or zero).
    uint8_t l4_offset = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_l4_offset(&idesc);
    assert(l4_offset > l3_offset);

    // Check for bad checksums and buffer errors.
    // NOTE: This checks "gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_status(&idesc)".
    assert(!gxio_mpipe_idesc_is_bad(&idesc));

    if (sim_is_simulator())
    {
      // This is rarely true on hardware.
      //--assert(idesc.packet_sqn == handled);
    }
    else
    {
      // This is only true if no other app has used the bucket yet.
      // FIXME: The "gp_sqn" is not implemented on the simulator. 
      //--assert(idesc.gp_sqn == handled);
    }

#endif

    // Normally we'd perform further processing/forwarding here.
    // For this example, we'll just drop the packet.
    gxio_mpipe_iqueue_drop(iqueue, &idesc);

    handled++;
  }

  if (is_dataplane)
  {
#ifdef DEBUG_DATAPLANE
    // If we had multiple workers, we'd want to use a spin barrier
    // here to ensure everyone's arrived before changing modes.

    // Take ZOL tiles out of "debug" mode.
    int result = set_dataplane(0);
    VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "set_dataplane()");

    // Again, if we had multiple workers, we'd want to use a spin barrier
    // here to ensure everyone's changed modes before moving on.
#endif

    // Unlock virtual pages.
    munlockall();
  }

}


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// main()
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

int
main(int argc, char** argv)
{
  int result;

  // --- command line ---

  // Set default link name based on whether we're on simulator or hardware.
  if (sim_is_simulator())
  {
    // On simulator, default to first simulated network link.
    link_name = "gbe0";
  }
  else
  {
    // On hardware, default to first GBE network link.
    link_name = "gbe1";
  }


  // Parse command-line arguments.
  for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i)
  {
    char* arg = argv[i];

    // --link <link_name>, used for egress.
    if (!strcmp(arg, "--link") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      link_name = argv[++i];
    }

    // -n packets -- maximum packets to process.
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "-n") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      num_packets = atoi(argv[++i]);
    }

    // --wait -- whether to wait for link to come up.
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "--wait"))
    {
      link_flags |= GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_WAIT;
    }

    // --tile -- CPU to affinitize to.
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "--tile") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      cpu = atoi(argv[++i]);
    }

    // --verbose -- print message for each packet received.
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "--verbose"))
    {
      verbose = true;
    }

    else
    {
      tmc_task_die("Unknown option '%s'.", arg);
    }
  }


  // --- CPU availability check ---

  // Task's initial affinity set.
  tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity(&cpu_set);

  // Dataplane CPUs, if any.
  tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus(&dataplane_cpu_set);

  // Determine default CPU to use, if not already specified.
  // Note: in general running IO main loop on a dataplane tile is preferred,
  // but we support running on a non-dataplane tile as well.
  if (cpu < 0) {
    // Use first dataplane tile, if any.
    cpu = tmc_cpus_find_first_cpu(&dataplane_cpu_set);
  }

  // Failing that, use first tile in task's initial affinity set.
  if (cpu < 0) {
    cpu = tmc_cpus_find_first_cpu(&cpu_set);
    if (cpu < 0) tmc_task_die("main(): "
                              "No CPUs in task affinity set.");
  }

  // Check whether we got a dataplane tile.
  is_dataplane = tmc_cpus_has_cpu(&dataplane_cpu_set, cpu);

#ifdef DEBUG_DATAPLANE
  // Sanity check -- OS calls like printfs will trigger ZOL debug output.
  if (is_dataplane && verbose) {
    printf("Warning: running on dataplane tile with ZOL debugging on.\n"
           "Disabling --verbose mode to avoid ZOL debugging stackdumps.\n");
    verbose = false;
  }
#endif


  // --- initialize mPIPE ---

  // Single ingress link, on single mPIPE instance.

  // Get the mPIPE instance associated with the network link.
  // This also implicitly validates that the specified link exists.
  mpipe_instance = gxio_mpipe_link_instance(link_name);
  if (mpipe_instance < 0)
    tmc_task_die("main(): "
                 "Network link '%s' does not exist.", link_name);

  // Initialize the driver for the mPIPE instance.
  result = gxio_mpipe_init(mpipe_context, mpipe_instance);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init()");

  // Open the link.
  // If --wait is specified, we'll wait for link to come up.
  gxio_mpipe_link_t link_in;
  result = gxio_mpipe_link_open(&link_in, mpipe_context,
                                link_name, link_flags);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_link_open()");

  // Get channel number associated with link.
  // NOTE: This is not used in this example, but would be if we were
  // egressing packets, or classifying packets based on their channel.
  //int link_channel = gxio_mpipe_link_channel(&link_in);


  // --- ingress queue (NotifRing and iqueue) ---

  // We use a single input queue to keep this example simple.
  // Typically, there will be one or more queues for each ingress worker.

  // Allocate NotifRing ID.
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_rings(mpipe_context, 1, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_rings()");

  // NotifRing ID:
  unsigned int ring_id = result;

  // Determine memory needed for ingress data structures.
  // Since we have only a single queue, we'll allocate a single huge page
  // to hold, in order:
  // - the NotifRing
  // - the buffer stack space
  // - the packet buffers

  // We put everything on a single page merely to keep this example simple.
  // Also, since we only have a single worker ingressing packets, there's
  // no real need to allocate/register multiple pages in the IOTLB,

  // Allocate memory for NotifRing, buffer stack space, and packet buffers.

  // Define memory properties:
  tmc_alloc_t alloc = TMC_ALLOC_INIT;

  // - input queue should be homed on same tile as ingress worker.
  tmc_alloc_set_home(&alloc, cpu);
  
  // - use huge pages
  tmc_alloc_set_huge(&alloc);

  // Allocate the page:
  size_t page_size = tmc_alloc_get_huge_pagesize();
  void* page = tmc_alloc_map(&alloc, page_size);
  if (page == NULL)
    tmc_task_die("main(): "
                 "Failed to allocate memory for ingress data structures.");

  void* mem = page;

  // The NotifRing must be 4K aligned, so we put it at the start of the page.
  size_t notif_ring_size = iqueue_entries * sizeof(gxio_mpipe_idesc_t);

  // Initialize iqueue and NotifRing, and associate memory with them.
  result = gxio_mpipe_iqueue_init(iqueue, mpipe_context, ring_id,
                                  mem, notif_ring_size, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_iqueue_init()");

  mem += notif_ring_size;


  // --- NotifGroup and buckets ---

  // We don't need to differentiate ingress queues/workers,
  // so we only need a single NotifGroup and a single bucket for this example.

  // Allocate a NotifGroup.
  int num_groups = 1;
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_groups(mpipe_context, num_groups, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_groups()");

  // NotifGroup ID:
  unsigned int group_id = result;

  // Allocate a bucket.
  int num_buckets = 1;
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_buckets(mpipe_context, num_buckets, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_buckets()");

  // Bucket ID:
  unsigned int bucket_id = result;

  // With only a single bucket, load-balancing isn't really an issue.
  // We'll use round-robin load-balancing since we're not checking flows.
  gxio_mpipe_bucket_mode_t mode = GXIO_MPIPE_BUCKET_ROUND_ROBIN;

  // If we had multiple buckets and/or we were looking at entire flows,
  // we'd want to use dynamic flow affinity instead, i.e.:
  // gxio_mpipe_bucket_mode_t mode = GXIO_MPIPE_BUCKET_DYNAMIC_FLOW_AFFINITY.

  // Initialize group and buckets, assign initial NotifRing to each bucket
  // and set the load-balancing mode for the group.
  int num_workers = 1;
  result = gxio_mpipe_init_notif_group_and_buckets(mpipe_context, group_id,
                                                   ring_id, num_workers,
                                                   bucket_id, num_buckets,
                                                   mode);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init_notif_group_and_buckets()");


  // --- buffer stacks and buffers ---

  // Allocate a single buffer stack.
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_buffer_stacks(mpipe_context, 1, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_buffer_stacks()");

  // Buffer stack ID:
  unsigned int stack_id = result;

  // Total number of buffers.
  // Use enough so we should never see buffer errors (idesc.be != 0).
  unsigned int num_buffers = iqueue_entries;

  // Actual space needed for buffer stack.
  size_t stack_bytes =
    gxio_mpipe_calc_buffer_stack_bytes(num_buffers);

  // Buffer stack memory must be 64K byte-aligned,
  ALIGN(mem, 0x10000);

  // We assume large packets (1500 bytes) at most, not jumbo packets,
  // and choose a buffer size accordingly.
  gxio_mpipe_buffer_size_enum_t buffer_size = GXIO_MPIPE_BUFFER_SIZE_1664;
  size_t buffer_bytes =
    gxio_mpipe_buffer_size_enum_to_buffer_size(buffer_size);

  // Initialize the buffer stack.
  result = gxio_mpipe_init_buffer_stack(mpipe_context, stack_id, buffer_size,
                                        mem, stack_bytes, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init_buffer_stack()");

  mem += stack_bytes;

  // Packet buffer memory must be 128-byte aligned.
  ALIGN(mem, 128);

  // Register the page containing packet buffer memory with the IOTLB.
  result = gxio_mpipe_register_page(mpipe_context, stack_id,
                                    page, page_size, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_register_page()");

  // Push buffers onto the stack.
  for (int i = 0; i < num_buffers; ++i)
  {
    gxio_mpipe_push_buffer(mpipe_context, stack_id, mem);
    mem += buffer_bytes;
  }

  // Check that we haven't run off the end of the single page.
  assert(mem <= page + page_size);


  // --- default classifier rules ---

  // Define a single rule that applies to all packets.
  gxio_mpipe_rules_t rules;
  gxio_mpipe_rules_init(&rules, mpipe_context);
  gxio_mpipe_rules_begin(&rules, bucket_id, num_buckets, NULL);
  result = gxio_mpipe_rules_commit(&rules);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_rules_commit()");


  // --- packet ingress ---

  // Ingress packets, up to limit set by num_packets.
  main_worker();


  // --- cleanup ---

  // Close/release mPIPE hardware resources.
  result = gxio_mpipe_link_close(&link_in);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_link_close()");

  result = gxio_mpipe_destroy(mpipe_context);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_destroy()");

  // There's no need to "release" context-managed state,
  // such as mPIPE ring/buffer IDs, IOTLB entries, etc.

  // In a long-running app, one would also free memory allocations
  // for rings, buffers, queues, etc.
  // but we're exiting so we'll just let these drop.

  return 0;
}
